2 - p. Bateman Of Mosler, ore.,
shab been selected as. one of the
judges at the national apple show.
The II . fruit grower Of wide re-
lpute. H. d. Van Deman Of Ferri-
|day, La, has also been chosen
Judge.


Henry. Lehman has been selected
Ebysthe expressmen of the city to
make test in the courts Of the
new regulation by the police and
railroad for the guidance Of hack
men at the N. P. depot. Lehman
has been fined 81 and will appeal.
Mrs. Ia Refine Baker, suffraoet
|ueader Of Spokane, was scheduled
to make an address at the A. Y. p.
fair today on behalf of the suffrage
movement In North Dakota.


Until further hearing Of the case,
ID. e. Young, the printing office
owner and real estate dealer, has
been required by Judge Hinkle to
pay his wife $1S5 as temporary ALL
mony. suit money and attorneys
fees.


2 c. N. Colbert, . Great Northern
brakeman, was knocked from the
platform Of his car while making a
flying switch and fell underneath
the caboose. He escaped with a
broken hip.


While he lay sleeping near his
engine at Washtucna, Wash, last
night, w. H. Taylor, a fireman for
the S., P. & s. railroad, was re-
lieved Of $45 in cash. A railroad
employee Of that place is suspected
and the case will likely be inves-
tigated by railroad detectives.


John Fuller Of Cheney, arrested
for his refusal to allow deputy of
County Health Officer Barnhart to
enter his home in Cheney to inves-
tigate a case Of chicken pox, en-
tered plea Of guilty before Justice
Stocker and was fined 81 and costs.


According to the school census
just completed, Spokane county has
2S,519 children OF school age, as
against 27,465 last year. The total
enrolment in the schools Of Spo-
kane county last year was over
$4,000.


r. L. Ford has purchased the
southwest corner of Second and
Howard street from w. H. Stanley
for $4,000. Mr.. Stanley a few days
ago sold three story building on
Sprague avenue to r. 8. Oakley for
$72,5OO.


Francis Cousins has disposed OF
q lot at the northeast corner of
First avenue and Adams street to
Malcolm McInnis and Hugh H. Nc
MILAN for $4,000. The lot fronts
en- both Sprague and First avenue,
is so fet wide and 1856 feet deep.


Clarence aMrsh, proprietor Of the
Lundbreck Coal Co., has filed suit
against the Alberta Fuel Co., in
which he demands the return of
$4,000, that he claims to have given
the company in the overpayment of
an account.
